title: ranunculus serpens subsp. nemorosus
intro: Ranunculus serpens subsp. nemorosus, also known as the Woodland Creeping Buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family, Ranunculaceae, characterized by its creeping habit and bright yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus serpens subsp. nemorosus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb for skincare and wound care. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be applied topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal benefits.
